titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the David Underdown Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday October 21, 2020 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 12 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: January 2013 acquisition; and June 2015 acquisition.

bioghist.head: David Underdown
bioghist.p: David Underdown (1925-2009) was a historian, author, and educator. Educated at Blue School, Exeter College, Oxford, and Yale University, Underdown wrote and taught extensively about 17th-century English politics and culture. Underdown taught at Yale University from 1986 until his retirement as Professor Emeritus in 1996. He also taught at a number of institutions including Sewanee College, Brown University, and University of Virginia. Among his many award-winning publications, his most well-known work was Pride's Purge: Politics in the Puritan Revolution (1981).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David Underdown Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below and titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The David Underdown papers, which span from 1930-2010, contain correspondence, lectures, teaching materials, personal papers, electronic files, and other materials created by or relating to David Underdown's life and work.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The David Underdown Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Writings, 1947 - 2010. II. Correspondence, 1940 - 2009. III. Teaching materials, . IV. Personal papers, .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, writings, teaching materials, ephemera, journals, and personal papers created by or related to the life and work of David Underdown.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings, clippings, reviews, drafts, and related papers and ephemera.

"Fire From Heaven" correspondence and related materials

Clippings and writings related to cricket match fixing
Notes and papers related to Watching Cricket in the Eighteenth Century
Riots and Revels paper and notes
Papers related to the Southern Historical Association meeting
Marshall University speech and related papers
"Fields, Forests, and the English Revolution" annotated typescript
Writings about William Gilbert Grace with related correspondence and papers
Paper abstracts from International Symposium on British History
Papers related to Church memorial lecture
Correspondence and notes related to AHA
Papers related to conference on British studies
Papers related to North East Conference on British Studies
"Seventeenth-Century Historians and the Revolution of 1640-1660" typescript
"The Problem of Popular Allegiance in the English Civil War" typescript
"Revisiting Inversion in Early Modern England" typescript
William Benn entry--Oxford dictionary of national biography
"Free-Born Englishmen: The Political nation in the Seventeenth Century" typescript
"Itching after popularitie: gentry politics before 1640" lecture typescript
"Custom and Inversion: Popular Politics before 1640" lecture typescript
"Liberty and Property: The Political nation and the English Revolution" lecture typescript
"The Man in the Moon: Levelling, Gender and Popular Politics 1640-1660" lecture typescript
"The Political Nation After the Restoration" lecture typescript
"But the Shows of their Street" peer reviews and related papers
"Yellow Ruffs and Poisoned Possets: Placing Women in Early Stuart Political Debate" typescript
"Chalk and Cheese" reviews with related papers
Papers related to award from Cricket Society
Papers related to dictionary of national biography
Corrected Ford lectures and related papers
Papers related to American Historical Association

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, much with other authors, along with related notes, clippings, and papers.

did.unittitle: Correspondence and papers related to 610 Angell Street residence
Correspondence and papers related to naturalization
Correspondence and papers related to Ann Underdown's estate and holdings
Correspondence and papers related to Susan Amussen

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains papers related to Underwood's experiences as an educator, including extensive notes on courses taught.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains journals, ephemera, and other papers related to the life experiences of David Underwood.

did.unittitle: Library cards, rail tickets, and other ephemera
Royal Air Force flying log book
Royal Air Force service and release book
Rolled photo of Wells School students

accessrestrict.p: Box 12 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
